mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns exceed zero in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE breaches 5.5%, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a statistically significant positive mean return in a multivariate regression controlling for market beta, or the interaction term loses significance in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and rising corporate output, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered months during recovery phases, or the autocorrelation of returns exceeds 0.3, indicating pure noise rather than supply-driven momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding employment and rising household income, which directly boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between payroll growth and 20-day forward returns drops below 0.05, or positive returns occur in fewer than 52% of months with payroll gains.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, the Federal Reserve is forced to tighten monetary policy more aggressively, raising borrowing costs and compressing equity multiples, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of high-inflation months, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for Fed funds rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid quarterly GDP growth combined with a rising federal funds rate creates an overheating economy where monetary policy outpaces real output expansion, triggering recession fears and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative returns with a t-statistic below -1.5 in out-of-sample tests, or the signal produces positive returns in more than 55% of high-growth-high-rate quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates improving labor productivity and supply-side efficiency, which expands profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields a negative mean forward return during productivity expansion phases, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a 5-year rolling window.
